title: Risk-Aware Selective Multimodal Driver Monitoring with Driver-State World Modeling
abstract: arXiv:2606.26922v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Continuous driver monitoring in automated vehicles requires low-latency inference while avoiding unsafe decisions under uncertain driver states. Large vision-language models provide broad multimodal priors, but their latency and limited reliability in this setting make them unsuitable as always-on in-cabin monitors. We propose a cost-aware selective inference framework for deployable multimodal driver monitoring. The core system is a lightweight RGB-physiological student that combines in-cabin visual observations with window-level HR/EDA signals, and a learned gate that decides when to accept the fast prediction or abstain for safety intervention. Additional controls show that the learned scores contain sample-level information beyond scenario priors, while exact physiological synchronization remains a limitation. To incorporate predictive evidence, we further study a compact driver-state world modeling module that rolls out latent dri

translated_title: 风险感知的选择性多模态驾驶员监控与驾驶员状态世界建模
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26922v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：自动驾驶车辆中的持续驾驶员监控需要低延迟推理，同时避免在不确定的驾驶员状态下做出不安全决策。大型视觉-语言模型提供了广泛的多模态先验知识，但其在此场景下的延迟和有限可靠性使其不适合作为始终开启的车内监控器。我们提出了一种成本感知的选择性推理框架，用于可部署的多模态驾驶员监控。核心系统是一个轻量级的RGB-生理学生模型，它结合了车内视觉观察与窗口级HR/EDA信号，以及一个学习门控机制，决定何时接受快速预测或放弃以进行安全干预。额外控制表明，学习到的评分包含超越场景先验的样本级信息，而精确的生理同步仍然是一个局限。为了整合预测证据，我们进一步研究了一个紧凑的驾驶员状态世界建模模块，该模块展开潜在状态。
tldr: 提出了一种结合轻量级多模态模型与学习门控机制的成本感知选择性推理框架，通过权衡快速预测与安全放弃来实现在不确定驾驶员状态下的可靠监控。
en_tdlr: A cost-aware selective inference framework combining a lightweight multimodal student model with a learned gating mechanism is proposed to achieve reliable driver monitoring under uncertain states by trading off fast predictions and safety abstentions.
